column1: # The four revisions of repair_tools/astor.jar in RepairThemAll's history.
# We pin e902cf5 (see build.env); the others are here so validate-astor-jars can
# check whether a different jar reproduces the published trajectory better.
# and checks it against the sha256 here. Cached in resources/astor-jars/ (gitignored).
# Columns: key  date  commit  sha256  astor_classes  note
# astor_classes = number of fr.inria.* .class files (rough "how different" signal).

column6: oldest; built matias/JDK9, 15.7MB, only 377 Astor classes (vs 853) -- a genuinely older Astor, the one candidate that could move a jGenProg trajectory
nearly identical to e902cf5: only 3 classes differ (ProjectConfiguration, ProjectRepairFacade, AbstractMain -- all setup/config, none on the jGenProg search/RNG path)
PINNED reference -- produced the published results (build.env)
post-experiment master rebuild; 8 classes differ, mostly Cardumen Cntx* context code + RandomSelection..Ingredient -- affects Cardumen, not jGenProg
